# crusoe_registry_repository (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "crusoe_registry_repository" "example" {
  location = "us-southcentral1-a"
  name     = "my-registry-repo"
  mode     = "pull-through-cache"

  upstream_registry = {
    provider = "docker-hub"
    url      = "https://hub.docker.com"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `location` (String) Location the repository is hosted in.
- `mode` (String) Mode of the repository, which determines how images are stored and served.
- `name` (String) Name of the repository.

### Optional

- `project_id` (String) ID of the project the repository belongs to. If not specified, the project ID will be inferred from the Crusoe configuration.
- `upstream_registry` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--upstream_registry))

<a id="nestedatt--upstream_registry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `upstream_registry`

Required:

- `provider` (String) Provider of the upstream registry.
- `url` (String) Base URL of the upstream registry that the repository caches images from.

Optional:

- `upstream_registry_credentials` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--upstream_registry--upstream_registry_credentials))

<a id="nestedatt--upstream_registry--upstream_registry_credentials"></a>
### Nested Schema for `upstream_registry.upstream_registry_credentials`

Required:

- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password used to authenticate to the upstream registry.
- `username` (String) Username used to authenticate to the upstream registry.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Registry repositories are imported using the format "<location>/<name>".
terraform import crusoe_registry_repository.example us-southcentral1-a/my-registry-repo
```
